content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. The only difference between this and the easy version is that here, you're given only N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 elements of AAA, and have to solve the problem for each value of the last element.**

Consider a tree on NNN vertices, with vertex iii having the value AiA_iAi‚Äč associated with it.

The score of the tree with respect to array AAA, denoted f(A)f(A)f(A), is defined as follows:


For each edge (u,v)(u, v)(u,v) of the tree, give it the value Au+AvA_u + A_vAu‚Äč+Av‚Äč.
The score of the tree is then the greatest common divisor (GCD) of the values of all the edges.
You are given a tree on NNN vertices, an array AAA of length N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1, and an integer MMM.

It is guaranteed that max‚Ā°(A)‚Č§M\max(A) \leq Mmax(A)‚Č§M.

For each x=1,2,‚Ä¶,Mx = 1, 2, \ldots, Mx=1,2,‚Ä¶,M, solve the following problem independently:


Append xxx to AAA, forming an array of length NNN where AN=xA_N = xAN‚Äč=x.

Compute the minimum value of f(A)f(A)f(A) across all rearrangements of this array AAA.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{N-1}A1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚ąí1‚Äč.
The next N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines describe the tree. The iii-th of them contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč, denoting an edge between these two vertices.




Output Format
For each test case, output MMM space-separated integers on a new line: the iii-th of them should represent the minimum possible value of f(A)f(A)f(A) across all rearrangements of AAA, if AN=iA_N = iAN‚Äč=i.
constraints: The input edges describe a tree on the vertex set {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N}.
The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM across all tests both won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
